title: Kerala Palada Payasam

ingredients.name: rice ada (thin rice flakes/wafers), rinsed
visual_checks: use store-bought Kerala rice ada/palada; rinsed and soaked 10 min
ingredients.notes: use store-bought Kerala rice ada/palada; rinsed and soaked 10 min

subs.name: thin rice vermicelli broken into 2 cm pieces for a lighter payasam
subs.notes: soak 5 minutes, reduce cooking time to 15 minutes

ingredients.name: full-fat milk
visual_checks: whole milk is essential — do not use low-fat
ingredients.notes: whole milk is essential — do not use low-fat

subs.name: evaporated milk (1 tin) + 500 ml whole milk for extra creaminess with less cooking time
subs.notes: add evaporated milk in last 10 minutes

subs.name: condensed milk (half tin) for caramel sweetness
subs.notes: add off the heat; do not reboil

steps.text: Rinse the rice ada under cold water and soak in plenty of water for 10 minutes. Drain well and set aside.
Pour the milk into a wide heavy-bottomed pot. Bring to a boil on medium-high flame, stirring to prevent a skin forming. Once boiling, reduce the flame to medium.

Add the drained rice ada to the boiling milk. Stir well. Cook on medium flame for 25–30 minutes, stirring every 3–4 minutes and scraping the bottom of the pot, until the milk reduces by a third and the ada is completely soft and has absorbed the milk around it. The payasam will look pinkish-cream as the ada cooks.

Add sugar and stir well. Continue simmering for 8–10 more minutes, stirring more frequently now as the sugar can make it stick. The payasam should be creamy and thick — it will thicken further as it cools.
Add powdered cardamom and rose water if using. Stir through and remove from heat.

Heat ghee in a small pan and fry cashews until golden, then raisins until plumped. Add to the payasam and stir. Serve warm or at room temperature. Thin with a little warm milk before serving if it has set too thick.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: poha → thin rice vermicelli broken into 2 cm pieces for a lighter payasam (soak 5 minutes, reduce cooking time to 15 minutes); milk → evaporated milk (1 tin) + 500 ml whole milk for extra creaminess with less cooking time (add evaporated milk in last 10 minutes); sugar → condensed milk (half tin) for caramel sweetness (add off the heat; do not reboil)
